4 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 6. (a) The director shall devote the director's
5 attention to the duties of the office during working hours and is subject
6 to call at all times. The director and any mine inspector funded by the
7 general assembly are authorized to enter, examine, and inspect all
8 commercial coal mines and facilities.
9 (b) The director shall have full direction of the official activities of
10 any mine inspector and shall be responsible therefor.
11 (c) The director and each mine inspector shall have power, and it is
12 their duty, to stop immediately the operation of any coal mine or part
13 thereof when any dangerous or unlawful condition exists. However,
14 where conditions exist justifying the director or the mine inspector to
15 do so, the director or mine inspector shall grant a reasonable period of
16 time for making necessary repairs. Where a stop in operation is
17 enforced, such director and mine inspector shall be empowered to
18 subsequently allow such mine or part of a mine to be reopened when
19 the dangerous or unlawful conditions have been remedied or removed.
20 If the operator or a workman believes that an inspector has acted
21 illegally in citing violations of mining law, they may appeal to the
22 director for relief from such citation. The director may grant or deny
23 such relief after a hearing, at which all interested parties have been
24 notified of such hearing and given an opportunity to present evidence
25 in support of their contentions.
26 (d) The director shall keep a properly indexed permanent record of
27 all inspections made by the director and the mine inspector, and copies
28 of all reports relating to coal mines shall be kept on file, and all such
29 records shall be open to inspection by the public, and shall be laid
30 before the governor at any time upon the request of the governor. The
31 director shall cause:
32 (1) within sixty (60) days of the date of the inspection, inspection
33 reports; and
34 (2) for two (2) years, all reports relating to coal mines;
35 to be posted on the web site website maintained by the bureau of mines
36 and mine safety created under IC 22-1-1-4(1).
37 (e) The director is empowered to revoke, in writing, any order
38 issued by a mine inspector for the purpose of stopping the operation of
39 a mine or part thereof. However, such revocation of an order shall not
40 be made unless and until the director has made a personal examination
41 of the mine or part thereof affected and determined it to be in a safe
42 condition to operate.
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

219
1 (f) The director or mine inspector shall make a personal inspection
2 of each mine in this state:
3 (1) at least once every three (3) months, or more often if
4 practicable, while the mine is in operation;
5 (2) whenever any danger to the workmen may exist; or
6 (3) whenever called upon to do so by the workmen.
7 During a regular inspection, the director or inspector shall have the
8 authority to inspect the surface plant; every working place in the mine;
9 all active haulageways, travelways, and airways in their entirety;
10 entrances to abandoned workings; accessible old workings; escapeways
11 and all other places where individuals work or travel; electric
12 equipment and installations; first aid equipment; ventilation facilities;
13 communications installations; roof and rib conditions; and blasting
14 practices, etc. The director or inspector shall have the authority to
15 measure the volume of air at the intake and return of the main
16 ventilating current and of each split, and the amount passing through
17 the last breakthrough in each pair or set of entries, and designate to the
18 mine foreman where the director or inspector shall measure the
19 currents of air as required by the mining laws of this state. In mines
20 operating more than one (1) shift in a twenty-four (24) hour period, the
21 director or inspector shall devote sufficient time to the second and third
22 shift to determine conditions and practices related to the health and
23 safety of the employees. The director or inspector shall make tests for
24 gas and oxygen deficiency in each place that the director or inspector
25 is required to inspect in the mine. Time shall be made available during
26 an inspection for interaction with the employees of the mine by the
27 director or the inspector to ascertain the familiarity of the employees
28 with self-rescuers and accessible escapeways.
29 (g) The director or mine inspector making an inspection of a mine
30 shall make an accurate report covering such inspection, showing:
31 (1) the date of inspection and actual time required to make the
32 inspection;
33 (2) the condition in which the mine is found;
34 (3) the extent to which the mining laws are violated;
35 (4) the progress made in the improvement of the mine, where
36 such progress relates to the health and safety of the employees;
37 (5) the number of fatal injuries and the number of nonfatal
38 lost-time injuries resulting from accidents in and around the mine,
39 and their cause; and
40 (6) in case any violation of the mining laws is found, the specific
41 section or sections violated, with recommendations for correcting
42 them, and the action taken to eliminate them.
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

220
1 (h) The director or mine inspector making an inspection of a mine
2 shall within three (3) days after the completion of the inspection
3 deliver:
4 (1) one (1) copy of the inspection report on the mine to the
5 operator, superintendent, or mine foreman of the mine inspected;
6 and
7 (2) one (1) copy to be posted within the three (3) day limit on a
8 bulletin board at a prominent place on the premises where it can
9 be conveniently read by the employees. If corrective action is
10 implemented, the report shall remain on the bulletin board for
11 thirty (30) days. If corrective action is not implemented, the report
12 shall not be removed from the bulletin board until the report of
13 the succeeding examination is posted.
14 The director or mine inspector shall keep the mine foreman or
15 superintendent informed as much as is practicable of any violation or
16 other unsafe condition as the regular inspection progresses. In instances
17 where, in the opinion of the mine inspector, an imminent or serious
18 disaster hazard exists, such inspector shall report the same to the
19 director by the quickest available means.
20 (i) It shall be the duty of the director and mine inspectors to enforce
21 the mining laws of this state and the mine inspectors shall perform such
22 other official duties required by the director as may be necessary to
23 secure full compliance with the mining laws of this state.

21 different interpretation of the building law or fire safety law.

25 commissioner:
26 (i) The name, address, and telephone number of the issuer of
27 the securities.
28 (ii) The name, a brief description, and price (if known) of
29 any security to be issued.
30 (iii) A brief description of the business of the issuer in
31 twenty-five (25) words or less.
32 (iv) The type, number, and aggregate amount of securities
33 being offered.
34 (v) The name, address, and telephone number of the person
35 to contact for additional information.
36 (vi) A statement that indicates that sales will be made only
37 to accredited investors, that no money or other consideration
38 is being solicited or will be accepted by way of the general
39 announcement, that the securities have not been registered
40 with or approved by any state securities agency or the
41 Securities and Exchange Commission, and that the securities
42 are being offered and sold under an exemption from
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

231
1 registration.
2 (F) The issuer, in connection with an offer, may provide
3 information in addition to the general announcement under
4 clause (E), if the information:
5 (i) is delivered through an electronic data base that is
6 restricted to persons who have been prequalified as
7 accredited investors; or
8 (ii) is delivered after the issuer reasonably believes that the
9 prospective purchaser is an accredited investor.
10 (G) No telephone solicitation is permitted unless before
11 placing the call, the issuer reasonably believes that the
12 prospective purchaser to be solicited is an accredited investor.
13 (H) Dissemination of the general announcement of the
14 proposed offering to persons who are not accredited investors
15 does not disqualify the issuer from claiming the exemption
16 under this subdivision.

1 (b) As required by section 2(27)(O)(ii) of this chapter, the Internet

16 (c) The Internet web site website operator is not required to register
17 as a broker-dealer if all the following apply with respect to the Internet
18 web site website and its operator:
19 (1) It does not offer investment advice or recommendations.
20 (2) It does not solicit purchases, sales, or offers to buy the
21 securities offered or displayed on the Internet web site. website.
22 (3) It does not compensate employees, agents, or other persons for
23 the solicitation or based on the sale of securities displayed or
24 referenced on the Internet web site. website.

26 and it does not hold, manage, possess, or otherwise handle
27 investor funds or securities.
28 (5) The fee it charges an issuer for an offering of securities on the
29 Internet web site website is a fixed amount for each offering, a
30 variable amount based on the length of time that the securities are
31 offered on the Internet web site, website, or a combination of the
32 fixed and variable amounts.
33 (6) It does not identify, promote, or otherwise refer to any
34 individual security offered on the Internet web site website in any
35 advertising for the Internet web site. website.
36 (7) It does not engage in any other activities that the division, by
37 rule, determines are prohibited of the Internet web site. website.
38 (8) Neither the Internet web site website operator, nor any
39 director, executive officer, general partner, managing member, or

41 site website operator, has been subject to any conviction, order,

26 (b) The application must be received by the division not more than

31 date of the order described in section 16 of this chapter.
32 (c) The application must be filed in the office of the division in
33 person, through the division's Internet web site, website, or by first
34 class or certified mail. If requested, the division shall assist a claimant
35 in preparing the application.
36 (d) The division shall accept all applications filed in compliance
37 with this chapter. Upon receipt of a complete application, the division
38 shall promptly begin processing the application.

23 reasonable time after the consumer's acceptance of the offer
24 to cure.

27 established. Not later than June 1, 2010, the department shall adopt
28 guidelines to implement the program. The program established by this
29 section, as implemented through the department's guidelines, must
30 meet the following criteria:
31 (1) The program must be available on a voluntary basis to
32 creditors that offer mortgages to Indiana customers after June 30,
33 2010.
34 (2) To participate in the program, a creditor must submit a
35 certification, on a form prescribed by the department, attesting
36 that the creditor qualifies as a five star mortgage lender.
37 (3) To qualify as a five star mortgage lender under the program,
38 a creditor must certify, on the form described in subdivision (2),
39 that the creditor meets the following conditions:
40 (A) The creditor offers or will offer to Indiana customers after
41 June 30, 2010, at least one (1) mortgage product that qualifies
42 as a five star mortgage under the program.
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

255
1 (B) The creditor does not have a record of any significant or

3 (i) IC 24-5-23.5-7; or

5 applicable to mortgage transactions;
6 as of the date of the creditor's certification. If the creditor is not
7 certain whether it meets the criterion set forth in this clause,
8 the creditor shall consult with the department before filing a
9 certification to participate in the program.
10 (C) The creditor does not have a director or an executive
11 officer who has been convicted of a felony involving fraud,
12 deceit, or misrepresentation under the laws of Indiana or any
13 other jurisdiction, as of the date of the creditor's certification.
14 If the creditor is not certain whether it meets the criterion set
15 forth in this clause, the creditor shall consult with the
16 department before filing a certification to participate in the
17 program.

19 mortgage must include the following terms and conditions:
20 (A) If the mortgage involves a purchase money transaction, the
21 mortgage must require a down payment by the debtor, or a
22 person acting on behalf of the debtor, of at least ten percent
23 (10%) of the purchase price of the dwelling that is the subject
24 of the mortgage. If the mortgage involves the refinancing of an
25 existing mortgage, the customer must have equity of at least
26 ten percent (10%) in the dwelling that is the subject of the
27 mortgage.
28 (B) The mortgage must have a fixed rate of interest.
29 (C) The mortgage must provide for an escrow account that:
30 (i) is established by the creditor, or a person acting on behalf
31 of the creditor, for the benefit of the debtor;
32 (ii) is maintained by the creditor, or a person acting on
33 behalf of the creditor, during the life of the mortgage; and
34 (iii) is used during the life of the mortgage to pay taxes and
35 insurance owed with respect to the dwelling that is the
36 subject of the mortgage.
37 However, this clause does not apply if, in the creditor's
38 ordinary course of business, the creditor does not regularly
39 establish and maintain, or contract for the establishment and
40 maintenance of, escrow accounts for the payment of taxes and
41 insurance, on behalf of the creditor's customers.
42 (D) The term of the mortgage may not exceed thirty (30) years.
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

256
1 (E) The mortgage may not include a prepayment penalty or
2 fee.

4 files a certification with the department under subdivision (3)

20 including any renewal certification required by the
21 department under subsection (b)(3), with the department
22 under this chapter; or
23 (ii) the creditor has filed a certification or a renewal
24 certification with the department under this chapter and
25 subsequently ceases offering at least one (1) mortgage
26 product that qualifies as a five star mortgage; or
27 (B) fails to comply with any program requirement;
28 the department, upon discovering the act described in clause (A)
29 or (B), shall immediately provide written notice to the creditor
30 that the creditor does not qualify for participation in the program,
31 or no longer qualifies for participation in the program, as
32 appropriate. The notice provided under this subdivision must
33 inform the creditor of the reason or reasons the creditor does not
34 qualify for participation in the program, or no longer qualifies for
35 participation in the program, as appropriate. Not later than seven
36 (7) days after the date of the notice provided to the creditor under
37 this subdivision, the department shall remove the creditor from
38 the list of creditors published on the department's Internet web
39 site website under subsection (c), as appropriate, and shall post,
40 on the same Internet web page on which the list described in
41 subsection (c) is published, a link to the notice provided to the
42 creditor under this subdivision.

30 (E) Oats and oat seed from June 1 to May 31.
31 (F) Popcorn and popcorn seed from September 1 to August 31.

34 31.

1 document that is maintained on a daily basis for each grain

6 title to the grain passes to the buyer and the price to be paid to the
7 seller is not determined:
8 (A) at the time the grain is received by the buyer; or
9 (B) less than twenty-one (21) days after delivery.

12 the buyer at a determined price; and
13 (B) payment to the seller is not made in less than twenty-one
14 (21) days after delivery.

12 (e) If the licensee's demonstrated current unencumbered assets is
13 are less than or equal to eighty-five percent (85%) of the required
14 amount or the licensee has not cured the unencumbered assets
15 deficiency as required in subsection (b)(2), then the director shall hold
16 an informal meeting in accordance with this chapter and, within thirty
17 (30) days of the conclusion of the informal meeting, issue either:
18 (1) a consent agreement that requires the licensee to take certain
19 actions within a set period, not to exceed twelve (12) months, to
20 remedy the current unencumbered assets deficiency, as the
21 director deems necessary and appropriate; or
22 (2) an order that revokes the license or licenses of the licensee.
23 (f) If a licensee, after an informal meeting in subsection (e):
24 (1) does not meet the requirements in subsection (e)(1), the
25 director shall revoke; or
26 (2) has an unencumbered asset deficiency that has continued to
27 decline, the director may revoke;
28 the license or licenses of the licensee.
29 (g) Subject to section 31.8 of this chapter, the director shall assess
30 a fine of one thousand dollars ($1,000) on a licensee that does not
31 maintain the unencumbered asset requirement under subsection (b).
32 (h) Nothing in this section precludes the agency from conducting an
33 on-premises inspection of a licensee at any time the director may
34 consider an inspection to be necessary or appropriate.

16 customer's child; and
17 (3) inspect the premises for dangerous hazards and defects, and
18 correct any dangerous hazard or defect within a reasonable period
19 of time after becoming aware of the existence of the dangerous
20 hazard or defect.

24 SECTION 284. IC 36-2-14-22.1, AS AMENDED BY P.L.225-2025,
25 SECTION 2, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
26 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 22.1. (a) As used in this section, "hospital" refers
27 to a hospital that is licensed under IC 16-21-2.
28 (b) Upon the request of a coroner who is conducting or will conduct
29 a death investigation on an individual who is admitted or was admitted
30 to a hospital, the hospital shall provide a sample of the individual's
31 blood or tissue to the coroner.
32 (c) A hospital shall have a blood retention protocol for the
33 preservation of the first sample of blood drawn for an individual
34 seeking treatment of emergency care services at an emergency
35 department of the hospital, regardless of whether the individual is
36 ultimately admitted to the hospital. The protocol shall include a plan
37 for:
38 (1) when a patient is transferred to another hospital; and
39 (2) notifying the laboratory for a requisition or order for whole
40 blood sample analysis to indicate when a patient is:
41 (A) treated for an injury that is suspicious, violent, accidental,
42 or from an overdose; and
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

290
1 (B) determined by a provider to be critically injured or
2 mortally wounded at the time the patient seeks emergency care
3 services at the emergency department.
4 (d) A laboratory that receives an order for whole blood sample
5 analysis and the notification under subsection (c)(2) shall do the
6 following:
7 (1) The laboratory shall hold the blood sample in storage until the
8 earlier of the following:
9 (A) The expiration of the twenty-one (21) day period
10 beginning on the date that the sample of the patient's blood is
11 drawn.
12 (B) The date of the patient's discharge from the hospital. For
13 purposes of this clause, the transfer of the patient to another
14 hospital does not constitute a discharge.
15 (C) The date of the patient's death.
16 (2) This subdivision applies if:
17 (A) the patient is transferred to another hospital; and
18 (B) at the time of the transfer, the transferring hospital
19 laboratory is still holding the first blood sample in storage in
20 accordance with subdivision (1)(A).
21 The transferring hospital laboratory shall hold the first blood
22 sample in storage in accordance with subdivision (1)(A), unless
23 the transferring hospital laboratory receives information from the
24 receiving hospital that the patient has been discharged from the
25 receiving hospital or has died.
26 The requirements set forth in this subsection may be waived in any
27 case where the sample is tested and cannot be retained for reasons of
28 medical necessity in the clinical care of the patient.
29 (e) A coroner does not need to obtain a warrant to request a blood
30 or tissue sample under this section.

4 SECTION 290. [EFFECTIVE UPON PASSAGE] (a) This act may
5 be referred to as the "technical corrections bill of the 2026 general
6 assembly".
7 (b) The phrase "technical corrections bill of the 2026 general
8 assembly" may be used in the lead-in line of a SECTION of an act
9 other than this act to identify provisions added, amended, or
10 repealed by this act that are also amended or repealed in the other
11 act.

13 SECTION 291. [EFFECTIVE UPON PASSAGE] (a) This
14 SECTION applies to publication of the following:
15 (1) A provision of the Indiana Code that is:
16 (A) added or amended by this act; and
17 (B) repealed by another act without recognizing the
18 existence of the amendment made by this act by an
19 appropriate reference in the lead-in line of the SECTION
20 of the other act repealing the same provision of the Indiana
21 Code.
22 (2) A provision of the Indiana Code that is:
23 (A) amended by this act; and
24 (B) amended by another act without recognizing the
25 existence of the amendment made by this act by an
26 appropriate reference in the lead-in line of the SECTION
27 of the other act amending the same provision of the
28 Indiana Code.
29 (b) As used in this SECTION, "other act" refers to an act
30 enacted in the 2026 session of the general assembly other than this
31 act. "Another act" has a corresponding meaning.
32 (c) Except as provided in subsections (d) and (e), a provision
33 repealed by another act shall be considered repealed, regardless of
34 whether there is a difference in the effective date of the provision
35 added or amended by this act and the provision repealed by the
36 other act. Except as provided in subsection (d), the lawful
37 compilers of the Indiana Code, in publishing the affected Indiana
38 Code provision, shall publish only the version of the Indiana Code
39 provision that is repealed by the other act. The history line for an
40 Indiana Code provision that is repealed by the other act must
41 reference that act.
42 (d) This subsection applies if a provision described in subsection
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

294
1 (a) that is added or amended by this act takes effect before the
2 corresponding provision repeal in the other act. The lawful
3 compilers of the Indiana Code, in publishing the provision added
4 or amended in this act, shall publish that version of the provision
5 and note that the provision is effective until the effective date of the
6 corresponding provision repeal in the other act. On and after the
7 effective date of the corresponding provision repeal in the other
8 act, the provision repealed by the other act shall be considered
9 repealed, regardless of whether there is a difference in the effective
10 date of the provision added or amended by this act and the
11 provision repealed by the other act. The lawful compilers of the
12 Indiana Code, in publishing the affected Indiana Code provision,
13 shall publish the version of the Indiana Code provision that is
14 repealed by the other act, and shall note that this version of the
15 provision is effective on the effective date of the repealed provision
16 of the other act.
17 (e) If, during the same year, two (2) or more other acts repeal
18 the same Indiana Code provision as the Indiana Code provision
19 added or amended by this act, the lawful compilers of the Indiana
20 Code, in publishing the Indiana Code provision, shall follow the
21 principles set forth in this SECTION.
22 (f) Except as provided in subsections (g) and (h), a provision
23 amended by another act that includes all amendments made to the
24 provision by this act shall be published in the Indiana Code only in
25 the version of the provision amended by the other act. The history
26 line for an Indiana Code provision that is amended by the other act
27 must reference that act.
28 (g) This subsection applies if a provision in this act described in
29 subsection (f) takes effect before the corresponding provision in the
30 other act. The lawful compilers of the Indiana Code, in publishing
31 the provision amended in this act, shall publish this version of the
32 provision and note that the provision is effective until the effective
33 date of the corresponding provision in the other act. The lawful
34 compilers of the Indiana Code, in publishing the corresponding
35 provision in the other act, shall publish that version of the
36 provision and note that the provision is effective on and after the
37 effective date of the provision in the other act.
38 (h) If, during the same year, two (2) or more other acts amend
39 the same Indiana Code provision as the Indiana Code provision
40 amended by this act, the lawful compilers of the Indiana Code, in
41 publishing the Indiana Code provision, shall follow the principles
42 set forth in this SECTION.
EH 1088—LS 6158/DI 112

295
1 (i) This SECTION expires December 31, 2026.
2 SECTION 292. An emergency is declared for this act.

Mr. Speaker: Your Committee on Judiciary, to which was referred
House Bill 1088, has had the same under consideration and begs leave
to report the same back to the House with the recommendation that said
bill do pass.

JETER
Committee Vote: Yeas 13, Nays 0

referred House Bill No. 1088, has had the same under consideration
and begs leave to report the same back to the Senate with the
recommendation that said bill DO PASS.
